[Affordability Confirmation] To Dana Lewis,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is funded. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be canceled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

Information provided to Together Loans by you:

Yours sincerely
Together Loans Applications Dept

Hi Kevin, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://togetherloans.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/OEBJ1Q8V - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Together Loans

Hi Tommy. This is Together Loans. We received your application from a third party broker, perhaps you were shopping around for a loan in the past few months and they thought we would be a good fit. We offer co-signer loans from $3,000-$10,000 & mainly serve those turned down by big banks or other loan providers. We're usually the cheaper option too! Our aim is to help those with lower credit scores build their credit history back up. A good co-signer is a friend or family member who knows YOU and trusts YOU. They agree to make any repayments you're unable to make - just like a safety net to catch you, in case you're ever in trouble. They need a good credit score, usually of about 750, and most co-signers own their own home. Continue your application here: https://tcrdt.com/s/1Z7QNTOT Thanks, Together Loans

Hi JOSHUA, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/PNE9ENBU - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. If you still can't find a cosigner, please reply CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Ben, Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0619082633030807071631] Hi Kim,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Transform CreditBuilder account when applying for a loan of $5000, on <DateCreatedCb>. We sent you a ‘Welcome to Credit Builder’ email on sign-up, confirming your account, how it works and how you can manage it.

Your CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: www.transformcredit.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $7000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.
 
Many thanks, 
Michelle@TransformCredit

Hi Joshua, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, so we should too! They can be ANYONE who has a 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Homeowners with slightly lower scores will also have a good chance of approval. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! Find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/OR4DILNT - we'd love to add you to the 40,000 customers we have already helped like you. [RE:Affordability Confirmation] Hi Rosalyn, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/W6VKYPPM - we'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

Hi Rosalyn, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 750+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/W6VKYPPM - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

Hi Marlena. 
We have two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer and CreditBuilder($368). 
The CreditBuilder is not a loan you receive, it is held for you by one of our corporate guarantors, and you do not owe money for this. 
We place the loan on your credit report and each $5 payment made is recorded on your credit history, which can help increase your credit score over time. 
It is optional and you can choose "Ok, set up my account" or "continue loan application without CreditBuilder". 
You signed up for the same on 06/26/24. 
You can check more information about the CreditBuilder here: https://transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder. 
To continue with your loan application, you can use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/DTPOSKB2. 
Thanks, 
Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0620221505230300031713] Hi Mikayla,

Thank you for letting us know you don't believe you submitted an application with us.

We received your application as cosigner for Marlena Manygoats $3000 loan over 36 months. Do you know Marlena? 
We want to highlight that at Transform Credit, a loan application is created only when a consumer applies with their details through our Transform Credit website. However, rest assured that we do various checks, before agreeing to any loan, and a loan could never be paid out without first verifying the identity of the consumer. We also speak to all customers over the phone or video before any funds are released so it's extremely unlikely your details could ever be used fraudulently to finalize a loan.
 
We can confirm that this loan application is now canceled, and no loan is paid out. In order to further protect you, we have also added a flag to this case so that should the application be attempted again in the future, it will be automatically blocked from progressing further.

If you have any more questions or need further support, we will be happy to help.
 
Best regards,
TransformCredit

Hi Jacqueline, thank you for contacting Transform Credit, your message is really important to us. We offer loans of $3000 to $7,000 over 2 to 5 years, depending on how much you will borrow. Our interest is calculated daily, so any additional payment could save you money in the long run. We recommend all of our customers pay as much as they can afford. You can make your payments before your selected due date and there is no cost for doing so. 
To amend your details and change your loan amount, you can use this link: https://transformcredit.com/details?aRef=0625080600200116111434
Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0625080600200116111434] Hi Jacqueline,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.

When you applied for a loan with us, we offered two different products: Loans up to $7000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly interest payments against, building up their credit score.

I can see that you signed up for a Transform CreditBuilder account when applied for a loan of $3000.

The transform CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. 
When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: www.transformcredit.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $7000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.
 
Have a great day, 
Michelle@TransformCredit

Hi Jacquie, based on your situation, we require you to provide a cosigner. A cosigner shows us that someone trusts you to pay, and that we should too! This allows us to lend to you even if you've been turned down elsewhere. If you could get a loan elsewhere without a cosigner, we might not be the right choice for you. If you have a suitable cosigner send them this link, they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/VEK62GE5 Reply CANCEL to unsubscribe. Thanks, Transform Credit

If you and your cosigner complete the application online and all checks are completed without issue, we could get the money to you sooner! If you need any help, just let me know. Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: 0608033000192219043433] I don't have a consigner for this loan application I'm a veteran getting ready to have surgery and this will help me out for the month I'm out of work on short term disability. What else can I do...

Yahoo Mail: Search, Organize, Conquer

[RE:Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: 0608033000192219043433] Hi Shawn, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/M1FCAW5Z - we'd love for your loan to add to the $25million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

[RE:Missing Subject] Hi Abdul, 

Thanks for reaching out, we understand that it can seem difficult to find a cosigner at first. Don't worry though, we help people like you every day!

We're a cosigner lender, all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign that someone trusts you, and that we should too regardless of your credit history! If your cosigner trusts you, then we will.

A cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor or even a colleague) who is a US resident, has a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time - someone who owns their home or pays a mortgage is much more likely to be accepted. Remember, as long as you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to make a payment! You might find some useful tips on how to approach finding or speaking to a potential cosigner here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans

When you have found someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/6L0R5FWI - we'd love for your loan to add to the $25million we've already paid out to people just like you. If you still find that you're not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this email with CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else.

Thanks, 
Jade@Transform Credit

[Your Transform Credit Cosigner Application Ref: 0605020222030518050727] Could someone answer why would the mo ey be sent to the cosigner instead if
me and the payments come from that person account

[RE:Your Transform Credit Cosigner Application Ref: 0605020222030518050727] Hi Abdul, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/6L0R5FWI

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Your Transform Credit Cosigner Application Ref: 0605020222030518050727] So who will the money be sent to and who account will the payment come out
of

[RE: Your Transform Credit Cosigner Application Ref: 0605020222030518050727] Hi Abdul,

We pay the funds to the cosigner for 3 key reasons:

1. To verify their identity and prevent identity fraud being committed against them.
2. To ensure they're fully aware of the loan (they can return the funds if they change their mind or see the loan and are unaware of it).
3. By accepting the loan to transfer to you, they're confirming their roles and responsibilities on this loan.

Your co-signer also agrees to make any payments you're unable to, so we can use these details as a backup payment method, but ONLY if you do not pay and the loan goes past due.

After the loan is paid out and transferred from them to you, as long as you pay on time your co-signer's bank information will never be used.

Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 060789DATE] Hi Patricia,

I am writing regarding your recent claim of filing for bankruptcy. As we have not received official confirmation, please provide us with your case number, the court in which you filed, and any other relevant information to help us verify your filing.

Until we receive this information, you and Patricia are still expected to clear the past due of $237.62 immediately at https://tcrdt.com/s/9V6ANC5K to avoid the full balance being due and risking litigation action against you both.  It is important to note that your bankruptcy filing does not remove Justin's liability for the loan.

Should we receive confirmation of your bankruptcy, it is in Justin's best interest to continue making payments. This will prevent any legal escalation, as mentioned previously.

Best,  
 TransformCredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan : 060789DATE] Hi Patricia, 

Thank you for informing us about your intentions to work with a Debt Management Company or Attorney to help manage your debts. For absolute clarity, we like to inform you of a few key points so that you can make an informed decision.

When you decided to co-sign for Justin, you agreed to be responsible for the repayment of the loan. This liability remains, regardless of any third-party involvement. As per your Guaranty and Indemnity, you promised to make any repayments Justin does not. You confirmed your understanding of these responsibilities and promised to make these repayments, prior to us paying the $4250.00 into your bank account. 

If you make payments through a Debt Management Company, those funds will be credited to your account. However, you remain responsible for making any payments Justin does not and so if there's a shortfall, or if payments stop altogether, the loan will still be reported as delinquent to both you and Justin’s credit file, negatively impacting your scores.

Further, should the loan remain past due, we would then look to pursue legal action against you both, to reclaim the owed amount. This could involve measures such as garnishment of funds or a lien on any property you own.

At this point, as we have not received any official confirmation that you are being represented on this debt, with contact information, we'll continue our direct communications with you regarding the status of your loan.

We understand that managing debt can sometimes be stressful, but we feel it is important that you are aware of the consequences of the loan falling behind for all parties on the loan. If you need any further help, please get in touch and we would be happy to support you as best we can to avoid the escalation of this debt. 

Thanks, 

Berginie Botero
Head of Collections at Transform Credit

[RE:Welcome to Transform Credit Builder!] Hi Dillon,
 
Thanks for getting in touch.
 
I can see that you signed up for a CreditBuilder account AND applied for a $3000 loan on 05/11/2024. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as payment for a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, your CreditBuilder account is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, TransformCredit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

You can find out more about the Transform CreditBuilder here - transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ . However, I can confirm that I have canceled your CreditBuilder account, you will not be charged again. 

We still have your loan application approved for you. All you need is a cosigner, a friend or a family member who has a 750+ credit score and agrees to make the payment if you can't. Follow this link to pick up where you left off: https://tcrdt.com/s/P05HN30F

If you need help, please let me know
 
Have a great day, 
Jade @ Transform CreditBuilder

[RE:You asked us to help with $10000 - and we can!] Hi Dillon, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/92HAVXDO

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0615022006240430283204] Hi Deborah,

Thank you for contacting Transform Credit, your message is really important to us.

I understand that you still don't have a suitable cosigner, but we can wait. 

Transform Credit is a cosigner-based lender.  This means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. 

Normally, a cosigner is a friend or a family member who knows you (the borrower) and trusts that it is a good idea for you to borrow money. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a strong history of paying their bills on time. A homeowner is more likely to be accepted.

How does the journey application work? When you apply online, we send you a unique link for your cosigner to register. As soon as we have everything we need from both of you, we’ll transfer the money.

Use this link to continue with your application: https://rgct.io/s/BTZVJ0MS

You can check here for more information about the journey application and common questions: https://transformcredit.com/faq 

But, if you are not able to find a cosigner, please respond to this message with CANCEL. You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Have a nice day,
Michelle@TransformCredit

[RE:Doreen has taken the first step in helping you!] Hi CHRIS, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 750
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/NJBBA5J3

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

interactions.body: Hi Melissa, thanks for your call. None of our agents are available, please reply here with your question & we'll get back to you. Thanks, Transform Credit

Hi Melissa, all of our customers must have a cosigner. It's a great sign someone trusts you, and we should too! They can be ANYONE who is a US resident, has 700+ credit and good history of paying their bills. Remember, if you pay on time, your cosigner will NEVER have to pay! You'll find tips here: https://transformcredit.com/cosigner-loans. When you do find someone, give them this link https://tcrdt.com/s/Z6KBLIBC - we'd love your loan to add to the +$100 million we've already paid out. If you still can't find a cosigner, please respond CANCEL so I can reserve the loan for someone else. Jade, Transform Credit

Hi Melissa. While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision. We can still pay out your $5000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. Just use give your new cosigner this link to apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/LYCJTO6G Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0625051403002501090906] Hi Preston,

Thank you for your contact.

While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. 

We have a responsibility to make sure that cosigning your loan is something that they would be able to financially support and based on the information provided we don't feel that this is the case for them. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision.

Our processes all follow applicable State and Federal laws and legislations that are applicable to our organization. Furthermore, Transform Credit Inc. is registered with the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), with license number 1905055. We welcome you to confirm this information on the NMLS website yourself: https://www.nmlsconsumeraccess.org/ We have annual state audits to maintain our licenses, and if there were any significant issues, we would be unable to lend.

Regarding your concerns over the use of your personal information, we do not use your or your cosigner's personal information for any purpose other than processing your loan application. Any information requested during the application process was only used to complete our identity checks and underwriting review in line with industry standards.
